sourceMovie.title: The Bank Job
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Bank Job (2008) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Roger Donaldson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Donnie Brasco
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Bank Job clicked for you, Donnie Brasco (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Bank Job?
faq.answer: Try Donnie Brasco, Payback, The Long Good Friday—each shares DNA with The Bank Job while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Bank Job?
faq.answer: The Bank Job (2008) is best described as Thriller, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Bank Job worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
